varieties.name: Sirora
varieties.origin: CSIRO Merbein, Victoria, Australia (released 1982 from Red Aleppo selections)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree, bred specifically for Australian Murray Valley conditions, red hull color, strong green kernel, biennial bearing tendency
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity - crops 2x heavier than Kerman, 3-6 tonnes per hectare across biennial cycle, dominant variety in Australia (97% of plantings)

varieties.name: Kerman
varieties.origin: Kerman Province, Iran (introduced to USA 1957, released commercially 1960s as seedling selection)
varieties.traits: Large spreading deciduous tree (6-10m), late flowering (late April), late harvest (September-October), biennial bearing pattern, requires 800-1000 chill hours below 7°C
varieties.yield_notes: Good consistent producer after year 7-8, 2-3 tonnes per hectare, moderate alternate bearing, industry standard for 60+ years

varieties.name: Golden Hills
varieties.origin: University of California breeding program (released 2005 from trials planted 1997-2002)
varieties.traits: More upright growth habit than Kerman (less pruning needed), early flowering (1-2 weeks before Kerman), early harvest (2-4 weeks before Kerman, late August-early September), potentially lower chill requirement than Kerman, reduced alternate bearing tendency
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yields in first 5 years of production compared to Kerman, excellent productivity, now comprises ~50% of California acreage (210,000+ acres)

varieties.name: Lost Hills
varieties.origin: University of California breeding program (released 2005, sister variety to Golden Hills with same parentage)
varieties.traits: Similar to Kerman in canopy shape and growth pattern (unlike Golden Hills' upright habit), early flowering and harvest (1-2 weeks before Kerman), less alternate bearing tendency than Kerman or Golden Hills, more consistent year-to-year production
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yields in first 5 years compared to Kerman, produces more consistently across years, reduces feast/famine cycle of alternate bearing

varieties.name: Gumdrop
varieties.origin: University of California breeding program (released 2016, newest commercial variety)
varieties.traits: EXTRA-EARLY harvest (10-12 days before Golden Hills, 24 days before Kerman), fastest growth rate of all varieties, thicker trunk requiring larger diameter rootstock, sticky 'gummy' husk (variety name origin), vigorous and fast-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Good yield potential comparable to other modern varieties, still establishing long-term production data as trees mature

varieties.name: Peters 🏆 (Male Pollinator)
varieties.origin: California, USA (released alongside Kerman, traditional standard male)
varieties.growing_notes: Industry standard male for 60+ years. Reliable and well-tested. Problem: in low-chill years, Peters often flowers AFTER Kerman, causing poor pollination and reduced nut set. Being replaced by Famoso in many orchards. One male pollinates up to 10-11 female trees. UNSUITABLE for humid subtropical climates/tropical monsoon climates climates.

varieties.name: Randy 🧬 (Male Pollinator)
varieties.origin: University of California (released 2005 alongside Golden Hills/Lost Hills)

varieties.name: Famoso 🧬 (Male Pollinator)
varieties.origin: University of California (released 2016 as Peters replacement)
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically bred to replace Peters due to its irregular bloom in low-chill years. Solves the Peters timing problem with Kerman. Modern orchards increasingly using Famoso instead of Peters. UNSUITABLE for humid subtropical climates/tropical monsoon climates climates.

varieties.name: Tejon 🧬 (Male Pollinator)
varieties.origin: University of California (released for Gumdrop variety)

varieties.name: Red Aleppo (Ashouri) 🏆
varieties.origin: Aleppo, Syria (ancient variety, cultivated for centuries in Syria and Levant region)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree, distinctive red outer husk that gives variety its name, medium-sized nuts, good productivity, adapted to Syrian semi-desert climate
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, reliable in appropriate climate

varieties.name: Batouri 🏆
varieties.origin: Syria (Batna region near Aleppo)
varieties.traits: Medium to large tree, whitish hull color, important Syrian cultivar

varieties.name: Antep (Gaziantep) 🏆
varieties.origin: Gaziantep (Antep), Turkey
varieties.traits: Small elongated nuts, distinct appearance, adapted to Turkish climate

varieties.name: Uzun 🏆
varieties.origin: Turkey (name means 'long' in Turkish)
varieties.traits: Elongated nut shape, smooth hull, oval-shaped fruit, thin shell

varieties.name: Kirmizi 🏆
varieties.origin: Turkey (name means 'red' in Turkish)
varieties.traits: Distinctive red pericarp (outer skin), oval-shaped fruit

varieties.name: Akbari 🏆
varieties.origin: Iran (Kerman Province)

varieties.name: Ahmad Aghaei 🏆
varieties.origin: Iran (newest commercial Iranian variety)

varieties.name: Kale Ghouchi (Kale Ghuchi) 🏆
varieties.origin: Bikaner district, Rajasthan, India (adapted from Iranian varieties)
varieties.traits: Thick shell, dark hull, small tree, adapted to extreme Rajasthan desert heat

varieties.name: Larnaka (Larnaca) 🏆
varieties.origin: Cyprus
varieties.traits: Short overwintering period (400-600 chill hours - less than Kerman), EARLY flowering (early April), medium-length fruit less elongated than Mateur, adapted to coastal Mediterranean areas
varieties.growing_notes: Successfully grown in Spain (Castilla-La Mancha), Greece, and Cyprus. Lower chill requirement than Kerman but still needs 400-600 hours. Early flowering. Grown primarily in Mediterranean basin. UNSUITABLE for humid subtropical climates (insufficient chill, high humidity) and IMPOSSIBLE for tropical monsoon climates (no chill hours, tropical climate).

varieties.name: Mateur
varieties.origin: Tunisia (selected for Spanish conditions)
varieties.traits: Short overwintering period, early flowering, elongated fruit shape

varieties.name: Aegina 🏆
varieties.origin: Aegina Island, Greece

varieties.name: Napoletana (Bianca) 🏆
varieties.origin: Italy (Sicily)
